data.narrative:
Quandela is a Series B quantum computing company founded by Valerian Giesz, Pascale Senellart, and Niccolo Somaschi. The broader quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable companies: 3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private entities including Q-CTRL, IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Quantum.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The quantum computing peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables; only one same-stage private company exists within the sector, falling short of the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for robust median triangulation. Expanding to all-sector peers would introduce uncontrolled heterogeneity in technology platform, revenue maturity, and capital efficiency, rendering any median-derived multiple unreliable for a Series B photonic quantum platform.

Given data constraints, conventional DCF and comparable-company approaches cannot be credibly applied. Valuation of Quandela would require either: (i) direct reference to transaction terms if disclosed by the company or its backers (e.g., PROQCIMA), (ii) founder-led pricing negotiations anchored to capital requirements and runway, or (iii) qualitative assessment of technical differentiation relative to the named peer set. Without additional financial or transactional data, no supported valuation range can be derived.
